title: symphytum officinale subsp. uliginosum
intro: Symphytum officinale subsp. uliginosum, also known as marsh comfrey or wetland comfrey, is a subspecies of the comfrey plant (Symphytum officinale) that is native to wetland areas in Europe. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and promote tissue repair. The health benefits of marsh comfrey include its ability to improve wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and other respiratory issues. Marsh comfrey can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote healing and reduce inflammation in the body.
